可能今次到我C

First we have to sterilise every equipment by either dry heating, autoclaving, chemical and UV light, and preparing the MS medium too, which include:

Sucrose,
vitamins
agar
macronutrient like nitrate, phosphate and sulphate, K+, Ca2+ and Mg2+
Micronutrient like Cobalt, Fe2+, Cu2+ etc etc
Ahh, sometimes something undefined, coconut milk and yeast extract.

And for the stuff that add after autoclaving, plant hormones such as auxin and IAA (Light-sensitive) have to be added, unless the explant itself can produce enough auxin The ratio and amount are very important, that auxin promotes root growth, cytokinin promotes shoot growth, but excess of both auxin and cytokinin will only give you a BIG CALLUS

replies.msg:
if it is intact plant, sow the seed into the agar
If it is callus, cut into pieces and place on solid medium high in cytokinin and auxin (e.g IAA), they will form big lumps of cells over the cut surface indefinitely to seal the wounds. If liquid medium used, we get small clusters or suspension.
These callus are able to re-differentiate into all part of plant bodies, even the whole plants.

If we treat them with cellulase and macerozyme to remove their cell wall, ta-da! Protoplasts~~~

Hey, don't forget changing the medium by subculturing regularly!
